intent: Privileged-command execution auditing (catalog #26): -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudo -F perm=x -F 'auid>=1000'. MEDIUM. The trap: -F perm=x expands to execve,execveat (permtab.h, f3 2.4) and this rule is EQUIVALENT to a -w /usr/bin/sudo -p x watch (f3 2.2) -- the cost model must price them the same.
suspected_trap: These syscall rules use -F path=...-F perm=x, which is EXACTLY EQUIVALENT to the watch form -w /usr/bin/sudo -p x (f3 2.2). A cost model MUST price the syscall form and the equivalent watch form the SAME (catalog #12 trap). Also: perm=x expands to execve,execveat (permtab.h, f3 2.4), so these are execve-class 1200 B events, NOT 900 B watch events despite targeting a path. The auid narrowing makes them MEDIUM (not unrestricted HIGH).
